message:
test(fr-conformance): XFAIL absolute-time TTL cases in core_strings live-oracle diff; close 18 beads

The `live_redis_core_strings_matches_runtime` conformance test drives
the shared `core_strings.json` fixture through both our runtime and the
self-spawned vendored upstream `redis-server`, diffing replies case-
by-case. A class of cases — those exercising absolute-time TTL
commands — has been flaky (read: consistently diverging) not because
either side is wrong in isolation but because the two sides use
different clocks:

- Our runtime honours the fixture's pinned `now_ms`, so `EXPIRETIME /
  PEXPIRETIME / (P)TTL` on a key whose absolute deadline was set with a
  small fixture offset returns the fixture-relative integer.
- Upstream `redis-server` walks wall-clock, so the same key returns the
  real absolute-time integer (or `-2` once the deadline is in the past).

For EXAT/PXAT variants the asymmetry is even more visible: a deadline
stamped with `fixture.now + 100ms` is already long in the past to a
real server, so upstream evicts the key and we keep it.

This is a shared-TTL-accounting problem tracked under
br-frankenredis-7rp6. Until that lands, the surrounding 200+ cases in
`core_strings.json` that aren't sensitive to absolute-time semantics
deserve active diffing against upstream — we shouldn't blanket-skip
the whole file. So:

- Build an `XFAIL` set of 13 case names covering the absolute-time
  offenders: pexpiretime_abskey, expiretime_abskey,
  pttl_whenkey_after_{expireat,pexpireat},
  pexpireat_whenkey, expiretime_whenkey,
  getex_verify_ttl_after_exat, getex_with_pxat,
  getex_verify_pttl_after_pxat, getex_with_persist,
  getex_verify_ttl_after_persist, set_exat_verify_ttl,
  set_pxat_verify_pttl.
- Load the fixture, filter case names to exclude XFAIL, and call the
  existing `run_live_redis_diff_for_cases(...)` helper (which already
  takes an &[&str] of stable case names) instead of the all-cases
  `run_live_redis_diff`.
- Update the test doc comment to explain the fixture-clock vs
  wall-clock asymmetry inline, and cross-reference br-frankenredis-7rp6
  as the unblocking work.

Net effect: core_strings live-diff is now strictly more valuable —
it actively covers every stable case against real upstream, and the
XFAIL set is a small, auditable list rather than a silent `return`.

Paired beads update in `.beads/issues.jsonl`:

- Closes 18 beads whose test-coverage or behavior question was
  resolved once the live oracle started exercising stable cases:
  frankenredis-1b89, 2hbk, 3cqd, 68ql, 6y8p, 9jl4, a118, ds9o,
  eud6, f3pv, gz9f, k2lp (+ others in the same batch).
- Reopens frankenredis-2di1 from `in_progress` back to `open` — it's
  the CONFIG SET numeric-wording bead and still needs a tighter
  parity pass before it can close; don't leave it mis-statused.

No product-code behavior change; this only adjusts which cases the
live-oracle diff asserts on, and housekeeps the issue graph.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
